danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming fire from hostile cybernetic entities; visual feedback when ship takes damage
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to player ship; failure state approaching

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual degradation or warning indicator when player deliberately slows ship
danger_signals.meaning: Mechanical danger: slowing down in the rail causes progressive ship damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, distinct enemy entity with expanded attack patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated difficulty checkpoint; major threat requiring skill execution

danger_signals.visual_form: Mechanical, artificial enemy designs contrasting organic environments
danger_signals.meaning: Foreign invading force; non-human threat to human survival

safety_signals.visual_form: Score counter; visual feedback when points reach upgrade threshold
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward ship upgrade; incremental power improvement

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual enhancement of player ship at stage start after collecting enough points (up to 6 visible upgrades)
safety_signals.meaning: Increased survivability and offensive capability; tangible progression reward

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition between eight distinct level environments with unique visual themes
safety_signals.meaning: Mission completion; advancement through campaign structure

safety_signals.visual_form: Receding geometric perspective similar to F-Zero racing tracks; hypnotic tessellation patterns
safety_signals.meaning: Forward momentum safety; staying on the defined path maintains ship integrity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player ship is implied human technology
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Asteroid belt setting (Mars-Jupiter zone); colonization frontier aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mechanical, artificial enemy designs; distinct from organic or natural forms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Inhabiting asteroid belt; corrupting/occupying 'virtually untouched by civilization' territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game setting: asteroid belt frontier instead of Earth; implied through narrative context
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory space colonization mission; survival imperative drives player action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy prevalence throughout all eight levels; density and complexity of hostile encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Relentless forward push (rail shooter mechanic); no retreat option; eradication mission

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Levels restart with same environments but player retains final upgraded ship and score
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating difficulty loop; no narrative endpoint; eternal defensive posture against infestation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Levels 1 and 3 graphics/music swapped between versions; boss color scheme misalignment in Western version
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Possible localization artifact or aesthetic preference; suggests content curation by region

environmental_storytelling.example: Mode 7 geometric tunnels, tessellation patterns, and hypnotic perspectives communicate advanced technology and alien/synthetic environments

Material Factory environment features flashing tetromino tessellations (Tetris reference); SNES controller-shaped boss with color-matched button satellites—communicates HAL Laboratory identity and meta-gaming culture
Eight distinct environments suggest different regions of asteroid belt infestation; each level's unique visual set implies varying cybernetic stronghold types
Landscape design and perspective sequences evoke transcendent sci-fi aesthetics; communicates intellectual, high-concept space exploration tone

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Takes progressive damage; mechanic forces forward momentum maintenance

entity_behavioral_tells.visual_form: Visual enhancements appear; ship displays six possible upgrade stages

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Shoot at player; block forward path; require dodging or destruction
Expanded attack patterns; require sustained engagement; gate progression to next level

notes: HyperZone communicates world-state primarily through narrative setup (uninhabitable Earth, cybernetic infestation) rather than dynamic visual feedback. Danger and safety are mechanically expressed: danger via collision/damage feedback and relentless enemy pressure; safety via upgrade accumulation and forward progress. Faction identity is minimal—human vs. machine is implied through ship aesthetic vs. enemy design rather than explicit visual markers (colors, symbols, uniforms). Environmental storytelling relies heavily on thematic visual sets (geometric sci-fi tunnel, tetromino factory) rather than narrative environmental cues. The infinite loop after level 8 visually communicates an unresolved, ongoing conflict state. Regional content swaps suggest localization decisions but do not affect world-state communication.
